preamble: The retraction of the voiceless alveolar fricative [s] to the voiceless retroflex fricative [ʂ]. This shift often occurs via assimilation to rhotics or as part of a systematic sibilant reorganization.

examples.note: The Ruki Rule: /s/ becomes [ʂ] after r, u, k, i.
The merger of /r/ and /s/ into a single retroflex fricative.
The apical [s̺] of Northern Peninsular Spanish is phonetically close to a retroflex fricative.

sources: Kristoffersen, G. (2000). The Phonology of Norwegian.
Penny, R. (2002). A History of the Spanish Language.
